slides.title: What is an RNN?
slides.content: Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) are a class of artificial neural networks designed to recognize patterns in sequences of data, such as text, genomes, handwriting, or the numerical time series data like stock markets.

slides.title: Structure of RNN
slides.content: RNNs are characterized by their memory that captures information about what has been calculated so far. This is implemented through loops within the network.

slides.title: Explaining Backpropagation Through Time
slides.content: RNNs use a learning algorithm called 'Backpropagation Through Time' to update weights.

visual_assets.code: def btt(time_steps, gradients): return sum(gradients[t] for t in range(time_steps))
visual_assets.caption: Python Example

slides.title: Applications of RNN
slides.content: RNNs are widely used in natural language processing, time series prediction, and other sequence tasks.

slides.title: Challenges with RNNs
slides.content: RNNs often face issues such as gradient vanishing/exploding and limited memory across long sequences.

slides.title: Improving RNNs: LSTM & GRU
slides.content: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) are popular RNN variants that help mitigate some of the challenges.

slides.title: Conclusion
slides.content: RNNs offer powerful capabilities for sequence prediction and analysis but require careful tuning and potentially sophisticated architectures like LSTM and GRU for optimal performance.
